channel.title: briefUpdatesHealthOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesHealthOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Telehealth abortion is in the courts. Share your experience.
item.description: Mifepristone is facing another major legal challenge.

item.title: Why supporting a shelter for women is now 'kind of radioactive'
item.description: That's how researcher Beatriz Garcia Nice describes the new U.S. stance under the Trump administration to programs addressing gender-based violence.

item.title: Despite Their Successes, Some Mobile Crisis Response Teams Are in Crisis
item.description: Mobile crisis units are trained to respond to emergency calls when people are experiencing delusions or hallucinations. But unlike police departments,...

item.title: Lawmakers, Health Groups Resist Their States’ Rural Health Fund Plans
item.description: Some Republican state lawmakers and state health associations are pushing back against spending plans under the Trump administration’s $50 billion fed...

item.title: As more people turn to chatbots for health advice, studies say they may be led astray
item.description: People are turning to AI chatbots to help them with medical advice. Recent studies suggest these bots are not always helpful in making decisions about...

item.title: A new one-a-day-pill holds promise for HIV's 'forgotten population'
item.description: It's designed to take the place of complicated, multiple drug regimens that many people with HIV need to follow. And it's also beneficial because the ...

item.title: Oren Nissim, Brook-ai – Figuring out RPM
item.description: Oren Nissim is the CEO of Brook-ai which is making some waves in the remote care space. Their goals is to use remote patient monitoring to help provid...

item.title: Listen to the Latest ‘KFF Health News Minute’
item.description: The "KFF Health News Minute” brings original health care and health policy reporting from our newsroom to the airwaves each week.

item.title: Even Patients Are Shocked by the Prices Their Insurers Will Pay — And It Costs All of Us
item.description: Health care prices are on the rise, and patients are flummoxed that even insurance companies aren’t doing more to control costs.

item.title: Hasta los pacientes se sorprenden por los precios que sus aseguradoras están dispuestas a pagar, un costo que al final pagamos todos
item.description: La inflación médica ha superado de manera constante la inflación general durante años, y las facturas de muchos procedimientos breves y de rutina lleg...

item.title: A homeless shelter and rehab center holds a marathon to get people back on their feet
item.description: Outside of Chicago, a homeless shelter and rehab center called Wayside Cross hosts a unique program to help people get back on their feet, one mile at...

item.title: Miriam Paramore, RxUtility
item.description: In this quickbite interview recorded at the Feb 2026 VIVE conference, I am talking with Miriam Paramore. Miriam is building RxUtility, which is helpin...

item.title: The Vocabulary of Survival
item.description: By GEORGE BEAUREGARD From 2018 to 2022, I served as a physician executive in a large health system on Long Island. During that period, I became acquai...

item.title: Will AI Solve Immunology’s Debate Over “Self vs. Non-Self?”
item.description: By MIKE MAGEE In 1872, English mathematician and sometimes poet, Augustus de Morgan, wrote this catching rhyme: “Great fleas have little fleas upon th...

item.title: Can We Ride the GenAI Wave Without Getting Subsumed by It?
item.description: By DAVID SHAYWITZ “There are decades where nothing happens; and there are weeks where decades happen,” said Lenin, probably never. It’s also a remarka...
